intro: Cyphostemma pseudoupembaense, also known as the African Milk Tree, is a succulent plant species native to East Africa, characterized by its thick, fleshy stems and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyphostemma pseudoupembaense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, with the tea being a common preparation for its medicinal benefits.
